reasoning: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.

concerns.analysis: The output contains numerous large, silent drops of pages and entire sections that are not accounted for by the initial '_omitted' flag. Significant chunks of the document are missing between elements (e.g., the jump from index 157 to 173 and 202 to 226). Absent content includes full pages such as Page 17 (Current Systems and Practices), Page 24 (Liaison Responsibilities), Page 28 (Hiring Practices), and several others.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Entire pages of content (e.g., Page 17 'Services Provided to the General Public', Page 24 'Language Access Liaisons responsibilities', Page 28 'Hiring/Contracting Qualified Interpreters for In-Person Communication')
concerns.output_quote: The element indices skip this content entirely (jumps from 157 to 173, 202 to 226, 232 to 242, 269 to 293, etc.)
concerns.location: Throughout the document, notably pages 16, 17, 21, 22, 24, 28, 31, 33, 35, 36, 37, 41, 42, 44, 47, 49, and 50

concerns.analysis: Two tables in the output contain header rows but zero data rows, despite the source document containing numerous rows for these action plans. Specifically, the Factor 2 action plan table (element 157) and the Section 3.6 action plan table (element 413) are empty in the output.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Factor 2 Action Items table (Page 16) has 11 data rows; Section 3.6 Action Items table (Pages 40-41) has 6 data rows.
concerns.output_quote: Element reading_order 157 and 413 show tables with header rows but zero data cells/rows.
concerns.location: Factor 2 Action Items (element 157) and Section 3.6 Action Items (element 413)

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: While most omissions are entire pages (C3), there are significant omissions within sections that are partially present. For example, element 128 (top languages list) omits 'Spanish', which is the top language listed on page 12 of the source.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The list of the top ten most common languages includes 'Spanish' at the top (Page 12).
concerns.output_quote: The corresponding list in the output (element 128) begins with 'Chinese', completely omitting 'Spanish'.

concerns.analysis: The table structure is corrupted in several places where the logical table from the source has been split across page boundaries. When these tables continue on new pages in the JSON, the first data row is frequently misidentified as a header row. For example, in element 140, the row containing Phase 'II' and its action item is marked as a header, despite being unique data that follows the actual headers defined in element 137. This occurs repeatedly throughout the document.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Phase Action Item tables continuing across pages (e.g., page 14, Phase II; page 19, Phase II; page 23, Phase I).

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: There are minor text omissions that alter the information content. Element 297/299 is missing the name of a guidance document, and element 547 is missing a lead-in URL reference.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: 1) '...Proposed Language Access Policy and Interpretation Waiver Form Guidance document.' (Page 29); 2) '(https://www.census.gov/topics/population/language-use/about.html). Based on...' (Page 54)
concerns.output_quote: 1) '...Proposed Language Access Policy and document.' (Element 297/299); 2) '. Based on the U.S. Census Bureau's group classifications...' (Element 547)

concerns.analysis: There are multiple list conversion errors. First, the Table of Contents (element 10) is rendered as a flat unordered list, completely omitting the hierarchical nesting of subsections (e.g., 1.1 under Section 1) present in the source on page 2. Second, lists contained within table cells (e.g., pages 38 and 45) are rendered as plain text strings using asterisk markers ('*') instead of being structured as proper nested list elements, losing structural meaning for assistive technologies.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Table of Contents on page 2 (nested structure); Action items with bullets inside table cells on page 38 and 45.
concerns.output_quote: Element 10 (flat list); Element 394 content: "...top five languages most widely spoken by individuals who use languages other than English: \n\n* Signage in visible locations..."

action_items: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.
